weather_system.philosophy: Weather is generated per region per day based on layered probability. Not scripted events. Weather has memory — dry weeks build drought, wet weeks build flood risk. The world's long-term weather patterns create short-term human crises.
weather_system.update_frequency: Daily standard. Hourly during active Breaking events.
weather_system.player_information: Player sees current conditions and immediate outlook — not a multi-day forecast. Skilled Weather Reading characters get a slightly better read.

tracked_variables.description: Regional baseline with daily variance. Expressed as a felt experience (bitter, cold, cool, mild, warm, hot, scorching) rather than a number.
Modified by season, prior days (wet days raise probability, dry days lower it), and regional geography.
Direction and strength. Affects sea travel speed and safety, fire spread risk, disease transmission, and the effective coldness of cold temperatures.
Affects temperature (clouds insulate at night, block sun by day), night sky visibility, navigation by stars, and morale.
Cumulative. Builds over dry weeks, resets with significant rainfall. When drought index reaches critical threshold during Scaldas, Reapfall harvest yield is penalized.

thresholds.dry: 21–40 — minor agricultural penalty, water sources shrinking
thresholds.drought: 41–65 — significant penalty, some water sources fail, fire risk high
thresholds.severe_drought: 66–85 — major harvest penalty, water rationing in some settlements
thresholds.crisis: 86–100 — crop failure likely, political instability follows

tracked_variables.description: Cumulative. Builds with heavy rainfall especially during Spring Breaking when snowmelt adds to ground saturation.

thresholds.flood_warning: 41–65 — low-lying areas at risk, river fords impassable
thresholds.flooding: 66–85 — settlements in flood plains take damage, road network disrupted
thresholds.crisis: 86–100 — significant settlement damage, bridge losses, crop field destruction

tracked_variables.description: Especially relevant in the Free Fens and coastal regions. Affects travel, combat visibility, and navigation.
Active only during Breaking events and severe weather. Determines specific event type rolled from regional Breaking tables.
active_during: Severe weather events outside Breaking (lower probability)

regional_weather_personalities.character: Long winters with sustained cold. Short fierce summers. Sudden violent late-season snowstorms even in Thornmelt. The spring thaw is dramatic and fast.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: Moderate — significant snowfall in winter, decent summer rain
notable_phenomena: Late snowstorms in Thornmelt catch travelers unprepared
River flash floods during spring thaw — the Durnwald River floods roughly 3 years in 5
Summer thunderstorms are brief and intense rather than sustained
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Moderate Spring Breaking, mild Autumn Breaking

regional_weather_personalities.character: Dramatic temperature swing between day and night regardless of season. Wind is almost constant — the plateau has no shelter from it. Rain is rare but violent when it comes.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: Low overall. Seasonal rains arrive in a short intense window during Thornmelt/Sowing that determines the year's water availability.
notable_phenomena: Dust storms build over dry weeks in Scaldas — can last days and make travel impossible
Seasonal rains after Spring Breaking expose new ruin sites
Night cold can be lethal to unprepared travelers even in summer
Wind direction predicts weather 24 hours ahead for those with Weather Reading skill
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Severe Spring Breaking (violent rains), mild Autumn Breaking

regional_weather_personalities.character: The most predictable weather on Arneth. Reliable seasonal rhythm. The exception is winter storm season — violent gales off the sea in Sternveld and Deepwarden that batter the coast.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: Moderate. Reliable summer dry season. Winter storms.
notable_phenomena: Sea storms in Sternveld can destroy harbor infrastructure
Drought years in Scaldas affect Solvane's food export capacity — rippling to Keth within weeks
The coastal fog in Coldmere and Thornmelt makes harbor navigation dangerous
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Mild Spring Breaking, moderate Autumn Breaking

regional_weather_personalities.character: Variable and often contrary. The borderlands sit at a meteorological crossroads — northern cold and southern warmth fight over it regularly. River flooding in Thornmelt is a recurring crisis that disrupts military operations predictably.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: High. More rain than most regions. Rivers run high.
notable_phenomena: Spring river flooding — the crossing at Cravenmoor's Ford is impassable 2 years in 5
Summer thunderstorms are frequent and severe
Autumn Breaking tornadoes — more common here than anywhere except open plains
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Severe Spring Breaking, severe Autumn Breaking — worst in the world for tornadoes

regional_weather_personalities.character: Wet almost always. The question is what kind of wet. Fog, mist, rain, drizzle, occasional genuine downpour. Summer is real but brief and precious. Winter is dark and wet rather than frozen — the Fens rarely freeze hard, which makes them passable but treacherous year-round.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: Very high. Driest month still has rain.
notable_phenomena: Dense fog events that last 3–5 days — navigation without fen knowledge is fatal
The summer fortnight in Solaran when the fog clears and genuine sun appears — celebrated
The Moor Communion considers the atmospheric conditions during Spring Breaking storms sacred
Standing water levels fluctuate dramatically — paths that are dry in Solaran are knee-deep in Thornmelt
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Moderate Spring Breaking (more flooding than storms), mild Autumn Breaking

regional_weather_personalities.character: Driven entirely by sea conditions. Clear and calm in summer, violent and unpredictable in winter. Storm season in Sternveld and Deepwarden is the most dangerous sailing period in the known world.
regional_weather_personalities.precipitation_tendency: Moderate. Distinct wet season (winter) and dry season (summer).
notable_phenomena: Storm season gales — ships lost every Sternveld and Deepwarden
The Autumn Breaking at sea is called 'the Argument' by sailors — combined sea swell and atmospheric storm
Waterspouts form in Scaldas — visible from shore, hazardous to small craft
The harbor bioluminescence is brightest after heavy rain — unknown cause
regional_weather_personalities.breaking_severity: Mild Spring Breaking, severe Autumn Breaking (at sea)

weather_cascade_examples.immediate: Water sources shrinking, fire risk elevated, livestock stressed
weather_cascade_examples.winter: Food stores 20–35% below normal — price increases, potential starvation in vulnerable settlements
weather_cascade_examples.political: Food price pressure creates unrest, desperate people take risks, bandits multiply

weather_cascade_examples.immediate: Crop destruction in affected area — 40–80% loss depending on storm severity
weather_cascade_examples.following_weeks: Emergency food purchasing drives up regional prices
weather_cascade_examples.winter: Potential famine conditions in affected region
weather_cascade_examples.political: Lord of affected territory under pressure — military recruitment may pause as men are needed at home

weather_cascade_examples.immediate: Roads impassable, river crossings closed, planting delayed 2–4 weeks
weather_cascade_examples.military: Campaign season start delayed — affects faction momentum
weather_cascade_examples.special: In the Ironmark Borderlands, Cravenmoor's Ford flooding may delay or prevent Brekhard's planned move

weather_cascade_examples.food: Stores plentiful — prices low, NPC morale improved
weather_cascade_examples.spring: People enter spring healthier, more willing to take economic risks
weather_cascade_examples.political: Stable lords become more confident, may pursue expansion
